response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain experience (founder of GISCollective, Green Map GIS work, FOSS4G speaker) and proven low-latency systems work at Sociomantic (DLang microservices handling millions of requests per second with async I/O and fibers), which maps conceptually to the pipeline and performance demands of routing tiling. However, modern C++ experience is brief and dated (a ~6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015), there is no Rust experience, and no direct navigation/routing systems background. Recent years have been predominantly TypeScript/Node.js web and product work, so ramping to a C++-first routing data pipeline role carries real risk despite strong engineering fundamentals and testing discipline.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial data experience via GISCollective and Green Map GIS applications
Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems work in DLang at Sociomantic
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source tooling, and knowledge sharing
concerns: Modern C++ exposure is minimal and roughly a decade old; no Rust experience
No direct navigation guidance or routing pipeline experience
Recent focus (4+ years) is TypeScript/Node web development, distant from the role's core stack
